name: Uganda People's Defence Force (UPDF) — ATMIS
detail: Largest ATMIS/AMISOM contributing country contingent (~6,000 troops at peak). Deployed in Mogadishu sector (Sector 1). Uganda has contributed continuously since the first AMISOM deployment in March 2007. Operates mechanized infantry, logistics, and engineering capabilities.

name: Kenya Defence Forces (KDF) — ATMIS Sector 2
detail: KDF has been deployed in Jubbaland since 2011 (Operation Linda Nchi). Responsible for ATMIS Sector 2 (Jubbaland/Kismayo area). Led the capture of Kismayo in October 2012. Approximately 3,600 troops at peak.

name: Ethiopian National Defence Force (ENDF) — ATMIS Sectors 3 & 5
detail: ENDF responsible for Galmudug (Sector 3) and Hiiraan/Middle Shabelle (Sector 5). Ethiopian forces have operated in Somalia since the 2006-2009 intervention and rejoined AMISOM in 2014. Temporarily reduced commitment during 2020-2022 due to Tigray conflict.

name: Force de Défense Nationale du Burundi — ATMIS
detail: Burundian contingent deployed in Lower Shabelle (Sector 4). Approximately 5,400 troops at peak. Burundi has faced criticism over alleged human rights abuses by its troops in Somalia.

name: US Africa Command (AFRICOM) — Somalia Task Force
detail: Approximately 450-500 US Special Operations Forces deployed in Somalia. Under Trump administration, the air campaign has been dramatically expanded: 57 confirmed airstrikes in Somalia by April 21, 2026 (AFRICOM PR #36382) — on pace for 200+ annual strikes, far exceeding the prior record of 63 total strikes in all of 2019. SecDef Hegseth granted expanded delegated strike authority to AFRICOM commanders. April 2026 targets include Al-Shabaab in Jubbaland (54 killed Apr 15–16; 57th strike Apr 21 near Wadajir), NISA ops in Middle and Lower Shabelle, and ISIS-Somalia in Puntland's Golis/Cal Miskaad Mountains (56th strike Apr 17). Operate from Camp Lemonnier (Djibouti), Mogadishu, Baledogle, and Kismayo.

name: Camp Turksom (Turkish Military Training Mission)
detail: Turkey's largest overseas military training facility, located near Mogadishu. Established 2017 under bilateral defense agreement. Has trained over 10,000 SNA soldiers. Turkey also operates Mogadishu's Aden Adde International Airport and manages Mogadishu port.

name: EU NAVFOR Operation Atalanta
detail: EU's counter-piracy naval mission, operational since December 2008. Provides convoy escort to World Food Programme vessels and deters/disrupts piracy in the Gulf of Aden and Indian Ocean. Extended repeatedly; mandate runs through 2026. Coordinates with NATO and Combined Maritime Forces.

name: Combined Maritime Forces (CTF-151)
detail: US-led multinational naval coalition; CTF-151 specifically dedicated to counter-piracy operations. Includes vessels from 34+ nations. Operates MSCHOA (Maritime Security Centre Horn of Africa) with EU NAVFOR to coordinate industry guidance and incident response.

name: SNA Danab (Lightning) Advanced Infantry Brigade
detail: Somalia's premier special operations unit, trained and equipped by US Special Operations Forces. Approximately 3,000 personnel. Danab serves as SNA's rapid reaction force for high-value target operations and complex attack response. Considered most effective SNA formation.

name: Somali Maritime Police Force / Coast Guard
detail: Nascent maritime security force supported by the UAE, EU EUCAP Somalia, and international partners. Responsible for counter-piracy operations in Somali territorial waters. Significant capacity gaps; dependent on international patrol vessels for offshore operations.
